instruction: Implement a RAII file handle wrapper in C++ that supports move semantics, provides a safe interface for reading and writing, and integrates with the standard filesystem library for path operations.
output:
<think>
RAII is the backbone of resource management in C++. For file handles, the key requirements: the destructor must close the file, copy must be disabled (a file handle is a unique resource), move must transfer ownership cleanly, and the interface should prevent use-after-close. I'll wrap a FILE* rather than using fstream because many C APIs expect FILE* and it gives more control over buffering and error handling. The move constructor needs to null out the source's pointer. I should provide typed read/write methods that handle partial reads (especially for network-mounted filesystems). Integration with std::filesystem::path means accepting path objects and handling encoding correctly. One subtlety: on Windows, fopen doesn't handle Unicode paths — I need _wfopen. I'll use a platform-specific open function. Error handling should use exceptions (via std::system_error) since this is modern C++.
</think>

```cpp
#pragma once
#include <cstdio>
#include <filesystem>
#include <span>
#include <stdexcept>
#include <string>
#include <system_error>
#include <vector>

namespace fs = std::filesystem;

enum class FileMode {
    Read,
    Write,
    Append,
    ReadWrite,
};

class FileHandle {
public:
    // Constructor: opens file, throws on failure
    explicit FileHandle(const fs::path& path, FileMode mode)
        : path_(path), mode_(mode) {
        const char* mode_str = to_mode_str(mode);

#ifdef _WIN32
        // Windows: use _wfopen for Unicode path support
        std::wstring wmode(mode_str, mode_str + std::strlen(mode_str));
        file_ = _wfopen(path.c_str(), wmode.c_str());
#else
        file_ = std::fopen(path.c_str(), mode_str);
#endif

        if (!file_) {
            throw std::system_error(
                errno, std::system_category(),
                "Failed to open: " + path.string()
            );
        }
    }

    // Destructor: closes file if open
    ~FileHandle() {
        close();
    }

    // Non-copyable
    FileHandle(const FileHandle&) = delete;
    FileHandle& operator=(const FileHandle&) = delete;

    // Movable
    FileHandle(FileHandle&& other) noexcept
        : file_(other.file_), path_(std::move(other.path_)), mode_(other.mode_) {
        other.file_ = nullptr;
    }

    FileHandle& operator=(FileHandle&& other) noexcept {
        if (this != &other) {
            close();
            file_ = other.file_;
            path_ = std::move(other.path_);
            mode_ = other.mode_;
            other.file_ = nullptr;
        }
        return *this;
    }

    // Read exactly n bytes, handling partial reads
    size_t read(std::span<std::byte> buffer) {
        ensure_open();
        size_t total = 0;
        while (total < buffer.size()) {
            size_t n = std::fread(
                buffer.data() + total, 1,
                buffer.size() - total, file_
            );
            if (n == 0) {
                if (std::feof(file_)) break;
                if (std::ferror(file_)) {
                    throw std::system_error(
                        errno, std::system_category(),
                        "Read error: " + path_.string()
                    );
                }
            }
            total += n;
        }
        return total;
    }

    // Read entire file as string
    std::string read_all() {
        ensure_open();
        std::fseek(file_, 0, SEEK_END);
        long size = std::ftell(file_);
        std::fseek(file_, 0, SEEK_SET);

        std::string content(static_cast<size_t>(size), '\0');
        auto buf = std::as_writable_bytes(std::span{content});
        size_t actual = read(buf);
        content.resize(actual);
        return content;
    }

    // Read line by line
    bool read_line(std::string& line) {
        ensure_open();
        line.clear();
        int ch;
        while ((ch = std::fgetc(file_)) != EOF) {
            if (ch == '\n') return true;
            line += static_cast<char>(ch);
        }
        return !line.empty();
    }

    // Write data, handling partial writes
    void write(std::span<const std::byte> data) {
        ensure_open();
        size_t total = 0;
        while (total < data.size()) {
            size_t n = std::fwrite(
                data.data() + total, 1,
                data.size() - total, file_
            );
            if (n == 0 && std::ferror(file_)) {
                throw std::system_error(
                    errno, std::system_category(),
                    "Write error: " + path_.string()
                );
            }
            total += n;
        }
    }

    // Write string
    void write_str(std::string_view text) {
        auto bytes = std::as_bytes(std::span{text});
        write(bytes);
    }

    void flush() {
        if (file_) std::fflush(file_);
    }

    void close() {
        if (file_) {
            std::fclose(file_);
            file_ = nullptr;
        }
    }

    bool is_open() const { return file_ != nullptr; }
    const fs::path& path() const { return path_; }

    // File metadata via filesystem
    std::uintmax_t size() const {
        return fs::file_size(path_);
    }

    fs::file_time_type last_modified() const {
        return fs::last_write_time(path_);
    }

    // Raw handle for C API interop
    FILE* raw() const {
        ensure_open();
        return file_;
    }

private:
    FILE* file_ = nullptr;
    fs::path path_;
    FileMode mode_;

    void ensure_open() const {
        if (!file_) {
            throw std::logic_error("File handle is closed: " + path_.string());
        }
    }

    static const char* to_mode_str(FileMode mode) {
        switch (mode) {
            case FileMode::Read:      return "rb";
            case FileMode::Write:     return "wb";
            case FileMode::Append:    return "ab";
            case FileMode::ReadWrite: return "r+b";
        }
        return "rb";
    }
};

// Factory functions
inline FileHandle open_read(const fs::path& p) {
    return FileHandle(p, FileMode::Read);
}

inline FileHandle open_write(const fs::path& p) {
    // Create parent directories if needed
    if (auto parent = p.parent_path(); !parent.empty()) {
        fs::create_directories(parent);
    }
    return FileHandle(p, FileMode::Write);
}

// Usage
int main() {
    // RAII: file closed when handle goes out of scope
    auto input = open_read("/data/input.bin");
    std::string content = input.read_all();

    auto output = open_write("/data/output.txt");
    output.write_str("Processed: " + std::to_string(content.size()) + " bytes\n");
    // output automatically closed here

    // Move semantics
    auto handle = open_read("/data/config.txt");
    auto moved = std::move(handle);
    // handle.is_open() == false, moved.is_open() == true
}
```

instruction: Implement a C++ thread pool with work stealing, dynamic sizing, task priorities, and future-based result retrieval. Support both fire-and-forget tasks and tasks that return results.
output:
<think>
A production thread pool needs several things: work-stealing for load balancing (idle threads steal from busy threads' queues), priority support so urgent tasks jump the queue, future-based results so callers can wait for completion, and dynamic sizing to handle bursty workloads. The architecture: each worker thread has its own deque (double-ended queue) — it pushes/pops from the back, stealers take from the front. This minimizes contention. For priorities, I'll use a priority queue per thread. For futures, I wrap tasks in std::packaged_task and return the associated future. Dynamic sizing: if all threads are busy and the queue grows beyond a threshold, spawn more threads up to max. Tricky parts: shutdown must drain the queues, work stealing needs careful locking (or lock-free deques, but that's complex), and the thread pool destructor must join all threads. I'll use mutex-based deques for correctness.
</think>

```cpp
#pragma once
#include <atomic>
#include <condition_variable>
#include <deque>
#include <functional>
#include <future>
#include <memory>
#include <mutex>
#include <random>
#include <thread>
#include <vector>

enum class Priority { Low = 0, Normal = 1, High = 2, Critical = 3 };

class ThreadPool {
public:
    explicit ThreadPool(
        size_t min_threads = std::thread::hardware_concurrency(),
        size_t max_threads = std::thread::hardware_concurrency() * 2
    )
        : min_threads_(min_threads)
        , max_threads_(max_threads)
        , stop_(false)
    {
        queues_.resize(max_threads);
        queue_mutexes_ = std::vector<std::mutex>(max_threads);

        for (size_t i = 0; i < min_threads; ++i) {
            spawn_worker(i);
        }
    }

    ~ThreadPool() {
        stop_.store(true);
        cv_.notify_all();
        for (auto& t : workers_) {
            if (t.joinable()) t.join();
        }
    }

    // Non-copyable, non-movable
    ThreadPool(const ThreadPool&) = delete;
    ThreadPool& operator=(const ThreadPool&) = delete;

    // Submit task with result
    template <typename F, typename... Args>
    auto submit(F&& func, Args&&... args, Priority priority = Priority::Normal)
        -> std::future<std::invoke_result_t<F, Args...>>
    {
        using ReturnType = std::invoke_result_t<F, Args...>;

        auto task = std::make_shared<std::packaged_task<ReturnType()>>(
            std::bind(std::forward<F>(func), std::forward<Args>(args)...)
        );

        auto future = task->get_future();

        enqueue([task]() { (*task)(); }, priority);

        maybe_grow();
        return future;
    }

    // Submit with explicit priority (simpler overload)
    template <typename F>
    auto submit(Priority priority, F&& func)
        -> std::future<std::invoke_result_t<F>>
    {
        using ReturnType = std::invoke_result_t<F>;

        auto task = std::make_shared<std::packaged_task<ReturnType()>>(
            std::forward<F>(func)
        );

        auto future = task->get_future();
        enqueue([task]() { (*task)(); }, priority);
        maybe_grow();
        return future;
    }

    // Fire-and-forget
    void execute(std::function<void()> func, Priority priority = Priority::Normal) {
        enqueue(std::move(func), priority);
        maybe_grow();
    }

    size_t thread_count() const {
        return active_threads_.load();
    }

    size_t pending_tasks() const {
        size_t total = 0;
        for (size_t i = 0; i < active_threads_.load(); ++i) {
            std::lock_guard lock(queue_mutexes_[i]);
            total += queues_[i].size();
        }
        return total;
    }

private:
    struct PrioritizedTask {
        Priority priority;
        std::function<void()> func;
        uint64_t sequence; // FIFO within same priority

        bool operator<(const PrioritizedTask& other) const {
            if (priority != other.priority)
                return static_cast<int>(priority) > static_cast<int>(other.priority);
            return sequence < other.sequence;
        }
    };

    void enqueue(std::function<void()> func, Priority priority) {
        uint64_t seq = sequence_counter_.fetch_add(1);

        // Round-robin assignment to spread work
        size_t idx = seq % active_threads_.load();

        {
            std::lock_guard lock(queue_mutexes_[idx]);
            queues_[idx].push_back({priority, std::move(func), seq});

            // Keep sorted by priority (insertion sort for small queues)
            auto& q = queues_[idx];
            for (int i = q.size() - 1; i > 0; --i) {
                if (static_cast<int>(q[i].priority) > static_cast<int>(q[i-1].priority)) {
                    std::swap(q[i], q[i-1]);
                } else {
                    break;
                }
            }
        }
        cv_.notify_one();
    }

    bool try_pop(size_t worker_id, PrioritizedTask& task) {
        std::lock_guard lock(queue_mutexes_[worker_id]);
        if (queues_[worker_id].empty()) return false;
        task = std::move(queues_[worker_id].front());
        queues_[worker_id].pop_front();
        return true;
    }

    bool try_steal(size_t thief_id, PrioritizedTask& task) {
        size_t n = active_threads_.load();
        // Random starting point to avoid contention
        thread_local std::mt19937 rng(std::random_device{}());
        size_t start = rng() % n;

        for (size_t i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
            size_t victim = (start + i) % n;
            if (victim == thief_id) continue;

            std::lock_guard lock(queue_mutexes_[victim]);
            if (!queues_[victim].empty()) {
                // Steal from back (opposite end from owner)
                task = std::move(queues_[victim].back());
                queues_[victim].pop_back();
                return true;
            }
        }
        return false;
    }

    void spawn_worker(size_t id) {
        workers_.emplace_back([this, id]() {
            active_threads_.fetch_add(1);
            worker_loop(id);
            active_threads_.fetch_sub(1);
        });
    }

    void worker_loop(size_t id) {
        while (!stop_.load()) {
            PrioritizedTask task;

            // Try own queue first
            if (try_pop(id, task)) {
                task.func();
                continue;
            }

            // Try stealing
            if (try_steal(id, task)) {
                task.func();
                continue;
            }

            // Wait for work
            std::unique_lock lock(wait_mutex_);
            cv_.wait_for(lock, std::chrono::milliseconds(100), [this, id]() {
                return stop_.load() || !queues_[id].empty();
            });
        }

        // Drain remaining tasks
        PrioritizedTask task;
        while (try_pop(id, task)) {
            task.func();
        }
    }

    void maybe_grow() {
        size_t current = active_threads_.load();
        if (current >= max_threads_) return;

        size_t total_pending = pending_tasks();
        if (total_pending > current * 4) {
            std::lock_guard lock(grow_mutex_);
            size_t now = active_threads_.load();
            if (now < max_threads_) {
                spawn_worker(now);
            }
        }
    }

    size_t min_threads_;
    size_t max_threads_;
    std::atomic<bool> stop_;
    std::atomic<size_t> active_threads_{0};
    std::atomic<uint64_t> sequence_counter_{0};

    std::vector<std::thread> workers_;
    std::vector<std::deque<PrioritizedTask>> queues_;
    mutable std::vector<std::mutex> queue_mutexes_;

    std::mutex wait_mutex_;
    std::mutex grow_mutex_;
    std::condition_variable cv_;
};

// Usage
int main() {
    ThreadPool pool(4, 16);

    // Submit with result
    auto future = pool.submit(Priority::High, []() -> int {
        // Heavy computation
        int sum = 0;
        for (int i = 0; i < 1000000; ++i) sum += i;
        return sum;
    });

    // Fire and forget
    pool.execute([]() {
        printf("Background cleanup done\n");
    }, Priority::Low);

    // Wait for result
    int result = future.get();
    printf("Result: %d\n", result);

    // Batch submit
    std::vector<std::future<double>> futures;
    for (int i = 0; i < 100; ++i) {
        futures.push_back(pool.submit(Priority::Normal, [i]() -> double {
            return i * 3.14;
        }));
    }

    for (auto& f : futures) {
        printf("%.2f ", f.get());
    }
}
```

instruction: Implement a C++ plugin system that supports dynamic loading of shared libraries, a typed plugin interface with versioning, and safe unloading with reference counting.
output:
<think>
Plugin systems in C++ require careful handling of shared library loading (dlopen/LoadLibrary), symbol resolution, ABI compatibility, and lifetime management. The core challenges: different compilers produce different ABIs, so the plugin interface must use a C-compatible interface (extern "C" factory functions). Versioning is critical — loading a plugin compiled against v1 of the interface when the host expects v2 will crash. I'll use a version number in the interface struct. For safe unloading: plugins may have allocated objects that the host is still using, so I need reference counting — only unload the shared library when all objects created by it are destroyed. I'll use shared_ptr with a custom deleter that decrements the plugin's reference count. The plugin manager should track loaded plugins, provide iteration, and handle the case where a plugin's init() fails (unload immediately). Platform abstraction: dlopen on Linux/macOS, LoadLibrary on Windows.
</think>

```cpp
#pragma once
#include <filesystem>
#include <functional>
#include <iostream>
#include <memory>
#include <string>
#include <unordered_map>
#include <vector>

#ifdef _WIN32
#include <windows.h>
#else
#include <dlfcn.h>
#endif

namespace fs = std::filesystem;

// --- Plugin Interface (shared between host and plugins) ---

constexpr int PLUGIN_API_VERSION = 2;

struct PluginInfo {
    const char* name;
    const char* version;
    const char* description;
    int api_version;
};

class IPlugin {
public:
    virtual ~IPlugin() = default;
    virtual const PluginInfo& info() const = 0;
    virtual bool initialize() = 0;
    virtual void shutdown() = 0;
};

// Plugin shared libraries must export these C functions:
// extern "C" IPlugin* create_plugin();
// extern "C" void destroy_plugin(IPlugin*);

// --- Platform abstraction ---

class SharedLibrary {
public:
    explicit SharedLibrary(const fs::path& path) : path_(path) {
#ifdef _WIN32
        handle_ = LoadLibraryW(path.c_str());
        if (!handle_) {
            throw std::runtime_error("LoadLibrary failed: " + path.string());
        }
#else
        handle_ = dlopen(path.c_str(), RTLD_NOW | RTLD_LOCAL);
        if (!handle_) {
            throw std::runtime_error(std::string("dlopen: ") + dlerror());
        }
#endif
    }

    ~SharedLibrary() {
        if (handle_) {
#ifdef _WIN32
            FreeLibrary(static_cast<HMODULE>(handle_));
#else
            dlclose(handle_);
#endif
        }
    }

    SharedLibrary(const SharedLibrary&) = delete;
    SharedLibrary& operator=(const SharedLibrary&) = delete;

    template <typename T>
    T get_symbol(const char* name) const {
#ifdef _WIN32
        auto sym = GetProcAddress(static_cast<HMODULE>(handle_), name);
#else
        auto sym = dlsym(handle_, name);
#endif
        if (!sym) {
            throw std::runtime_error(
                std::string("Symbol not found: ") + name + " in " + path_.string()
            );
        }
        return reinterpret_cast<T>(sym);
    }

    const fs::path& path() const { return path_; }

private:
    void* handle_ = nullptr;
    fs::path path_;
};

// --- Plugin Handle with reference counting ---

class PluginHandle {
public:
    using CreateFn = IPlugin* (*)();
    using DestroyFn = void (*)(IPlugin*);

    PluginHandle(std::unique_ptr<SharedLibrary> lib)
        : library_(std::move(lib))
        , ref_count_(0)
    {
        create_fn_ = library_->get_symbol<CreateFn>("create_plugin");
        destroy_fn_ = library_->get_symbol<DestroyFn>("destroy_plugin");

        // Create instance
        raw_plugin_ = create_fn_();
        if (!raw_plugin_) {
            throw std::runtime_error("create_plugin returned null");
        }

        // Version check
        if (raw_plugin_->info().api_version != PLUGIN_API_VERSION) {
            destroy_fn_(raw_plugin_);
            throw std::runtime_error(
                std::string("API version mismatch: plugin has v") +
                std::to_string(raw_plugin_->info().api_version) +
                ", host expects v" + std::to_string(PLUGIN_API_VERSION)
            );
        }
    }

    ~PluginHandle() {
        if (raw_plugin_) {
            raw_plugin_->shutdown();
            destroy_fn_(raw_plugin_);
        }
    }

    IPlugin* get() const { return raw_plugin_; }
    const PluginInfo& info() const { return raw_plugin_->info(); }

    // Reference counting for safe unload
    void add_ref() { ++ref_count_; }
    void release() { --ref_count_; }
    int ref_count() const { return ref_count_.load(); }
    bool can_unload() const { return ref_count_.load() == 0; }

private:
    std::unique_ptr<SharedLibrary> library_;
    IPlugin* raw_plugin_ = nullptr;
    CreateFn create_fn_ = nullptr;
    DestroyFn destroy_fn_ = nullptr;
    std::atomic<int> ref_count_;
};

// --- Ref-counted plugin pointer ---

class PluginRef {
public:
    PluginRef() = default;

    explicit PluginRef(std::shared_ptr<PluginHandle> handle)
        : handle_(std::move(handle)) {
        if (handle_) handle_->add_ref();
    }

    ~PluginRef() {
        if (handle_) handle_->release();
    }

    PluginRef(const PluginRef& other) : handle_(other.handle_) {
        if (handle_) handle_->add_ref();
    }

    PluginRef& operator=(const PluginRef& other) {
        if (this != &other) {
            if (handle_) handle_->release();
            handle_ = other.handle_;
            if (handle_) handle_->add_ref();
        }
        return *this;
    }

    IPlugin* operator->() const { return handle_->get(); }
    IPlugin& operator*() const { return *handle_->get(); }
    explicit operator bool() const { return handle_ != nullptr; }

private:
    std::shared_ptr<PluginHandle> handle_;
};

// --- Plugin Manager ---

class PluginManager {
public:
    PluginRef load(const fs::path& path) {
        auto key = fs::canonical(path).string();

        if (auto it = plugins_.find(key); it != plugins_.end()) {
            return PluginRef(it->second);
        }

        auto lib = std::make_unique<SharedLibrary>(path);
        auto handle = std::make_shared<PluginHandle>(std::move(lib));

        if (!handle->get()->initialize()) {
            throw std::runtime_error(
                "Plugin initialization failed: " + std::string(handle->info().name)
            );
        }

        std::cout << "[plugins] Loaded: " << handle->info().name
                  << " v" << handle->info().version << "\n";

        plugins_[key] = handle;
        return PluginRef(handle);
    }

    bool unload(const std::string& name) {
        for (auto it = plugins_.begin(); it != plugins_.end(); ++it) {
            if (it->second->info().name == name) {
                if (!it->second->can_unload()) {
                    std::cerr << "[plugins] Cannot unload '" << name
                              << "': " << it->second->ref_count() << " refs\n";
                    return false;
                }
                plugins_.erase(it);
                std::cout << "[plugins] Unloaded: " << name << "\n";
                return true;
            }
        }
        return false;
    }

    void load_directory(const fs::path& dir) {
        for (const auto& entry : fs::directory_iterator(dir)) {
            auto ext = entry.path().extension();
            if (ext == ".so" || ext == ".dll" || ext == ".dylib") {
                try {
                    load(entry.path());
                } catch (const std::exception& e) {
                    std::cerr << "[plugins] Failed to load "
                              << entry.path() << ": " << e.what() << "\n";
                }
            }
        }
    }

    std::vector<PluginRef> all() const {
        std::vector<PluginRef> result;
        for (const auto& [_, handle] : plugins_) {
            result.emplace_back(handle);
        }
        return result;
    }

private:
    std::unordered_map<std::string, std::shared_ptr<PluginHandle>> plugins_;
};
```

instruction: Implement a C++ memory-mapped I/O wrapper that supports read/write access, automatic unmapping via RAII, region views for partial mapping, and cross-platform support (Linux and Windows).
output:
<think>
Memory-mapped I/O in C++ requires platform-specific system calls: mmap on Linux/macOS and MapViewOfFile on Windows. The RAII wrapper needs to handle: opening the file, creating the mapping, creating views into specific regions, and cleaning up in the right order (unmap view → close mapping → close file). For cross-platform support, I'll use #ifdef _WIN32 throughout. Key design decisions: support both read-only and read-write modes, allow mapping sub-regions of the file (important for huge files — map only what you need), and provide typed access (interpret the mapped region as an array of T). The tricky part is alignment: on both platforms, the offset must be aligned to the allocation granularity (64KB on Windows, page size on Linux). I need to handle this transparently — if the user requests an unaligned offset, round down and adjust the returned pointer. RAII cleanup must happen in the destructor, and the class should be movable but not copyable.
</think>

```cpp
#pragma once
#include <cstddef>
#include <cstdint>
#include <filesystem>
#include <span>
#include <stdexcept>
#include <string>
#include <utility>

#ifdef _WIN32
#include <windows.h>
#else
#include <fcntl.h>
#include <sys/mman.h>
#include <sys/stat.h>
#include <unistd.h>
#endif

namespace fs = std::filesystem;

enum class MapMode { ReadOnly, ReadWrite };

class MappedFile {
public:
    MappedFile(const fs::path& path, MapMode mode)
        : mode_(mode), file_size_(fs::file_size(path))
    {
#ifdef _WIN32
        DWORD access = (mode == MapMode::ReadWrite) ? GENERIC_READ | GENERIC_WRITE : GENERIC_READ;
        DWORD share = FILE_SHARE_READ;
        file_handle_ = CreateFileW(
            path.c_str(), access, share, nullptr,
            OPEN_EXISTING, FILE_ATTRIBUTE_NORMAL, nullptr
        );
        if (file_handle_ == INVALID_HANDLE_VALUE) {
            throw std::runtime_error("CreateFile failed: " + path.string());
        }

        DWORD protect = (mode == MapMode::ReadWrite) ? PAGE_READWRITE : PAGE_READONLY;
        mapping_handle_ = CreateFileMappingW(
            file_handle_, nullptr, protect, 0, 0, nullptr
        );
        if (!mapping_handle_) {
            CloseHandle(file_handle_);
            throw std::runtime_error("CreateFileMapping failed: " + path.string());
        }

        DWORD map_access = (mode == MapMode::ReadWrite) ? FILE_MAP_ALL_ACCESS : FILE_MAP_READ;
        data_ = static_cast<std::byte*>(MapViewOfFile(
            mapping_handle_, map_access, 0, 0, 0
        ));
        if (!data_) {
            CloseHandle(mapping_handle_);
            CloseHandle(file_handle_);
            throw std::runtime_error("MapViewOfFile failed: " + path.string());
        }
#else
        int flags = (mode == MapMode::ReadWrite) ? O_RDWR : O_RDONLY;
        fd_ = open(path.c_str(), flags);
        if (fd_ < 0) {
            throw std::runtime_error("open failed: " + path.string());
        }

        int prot = (mode == MapMode::ReadWrite) ? (PROT_READ | PROT_WRITE) : PROT_READ;
        data_ = static_cast<std::byte*>(mmap(
            nullptr, file_size_, prot, MAP_SHARED, fd_, 0
        ));
        if (data_ == MAP_FAILED) {
            close(fd_);
            data_ = nullptr;
            throw std::runtime_error("mmap failed: " + path.string());
        }
#endif
    }

    ~MappedFile() { unmap(); }

    // Non-copyable
    MappedFile(const MappedFile&) = delete;
    MappedFile& operator=(const MappedFile&) = delete;

    // Movable
    MappedFile(MappedFile&& other) noexcept
        : data_(other.data_), file_size_(other.file_size_), mode_(other.mode_)
    {
#ifdef _WIN32
        file_handle_ = other.file_handle_;
        mapping_handle_ = other.mapping_handle_;
        other.file_handle_ = INVALID_HANDLE_VALUE;
        other.mapping_handle_ = nullptr;
#else
        fd_ = other.fd_;
        other.fd_ = -1;
#endif
        other.data_ = nullptr;
        other.file_size_ = 0;
    }

    MappedFile& operator=(MappedFile&& other) noexcept {
        if (this != &other) {
            unmap();
            data_ = other.data_;
            file_size_ = other.file_size_;
            mode_ = other.mode_;
#ifdef _WIN32
            file_handle_ = other.file_handle_;
            mapping_handle_ = other.mapping_handle_;
            other.file_handle_ = INVALID_HANDLE_VALUE;
            other.mapping_handle_ = nullptr;
#else
            fd_ = other.fd_;
            other.fd_ = -1;
#endif
            other.data_ = nullptr;
            other.file_size_ = 0;
        }
        return *this;
    }

    // Raw access
    std::byte* data() { return data_; }
    const std::byte* data() const { return data_; }
    size_t size() const { return file_size_; }

    // Span-based access
    std::span<std::byte> span() {
        return {data_, file_size_};
    }
    std::span<const std::byte> span() const {
        return {data_, file_size_};
    }

    // Region view
    std::span<std::byte> region(size_t offset, size_t length) {
        if (offset + length > file_size_) {
            throw std::out_of_range("Region exceeds file size");
        }
        return {data_ + offset, length};
    }

    // Typed view: interpret region as array of T
    template <typename T>
    std::span<T> as(size_t byte_offset = 0) {
        if (byte_offset % alignof(T) != 0) {
            throw std::runtime_error("Unaligned access for type");
        }
        size_t count = (file_size_ - byte_offset) / sizeof(T);
        return {reinterpret_cast<T*>(data_ + byte_offset), count};
    }

    template <typename T>
    std::span<const T> as(size_t byte_offset = 0) const {
        if (byte_offset % alignof(T) != 0) {
            throw std::runtime_error("Unaligned access for type");
        }
        size_t count = (file_size_ - byte_offset) / sizeof(T);
        return {reinterpret_cast<const T*>(data_ + byte_offset), count};
    }

    // Flush changes to disk
    void flush(size_t offset = 0, size_t length = 0) {
        if (length == 0) length = file_size_;
#ifdef _WIN32
        FlushViewOfFile(data_ + offset, length);
#else
        msync(data_ + offset, length, MS_SYNC);
#endif
    }

private:
    void unmap() {
        if (!data_) return;
#ifdef _WIN32
        UnmapViewOfFile(data_);
        if (mapping_handle_) CloseHandle(mapping_handle_);
        if (file_handle_ != INVALID_HANDLE_VALUE) CloseHandle(file_handle_);
        file_handle_ = INVALID_HANDLE_VALUE;
        mapping_handle_ = nullptr;
#else
        munmap(data_, file_size_);
        if (fd_ >= 0) ::close(fd_);
        fd_ = -1;
#endif
        data_ = nullptr;
    }

    std::byte* data_ = nullptr;
    size_t file_size_ = 0;
    MapMode mode_;

#ifdef _WIN32
    HANDLE file_handle_ = INVALID_HANDLE_VALUE;
    HANDLE mapping_handle_ = nullptr;
#else
    int fd_ = -1;
#endif
};

// Usage
int main() {
    // Read-only mapping
    {
        MappedFile file("/data/large_dataset.bin", MapMode::ReadOnly);
        auto records = file.as<const double>(0);
        double sum = 0;
        for (double v : records) sum += v;
        printf("Sum of %zu doubles: %f\n", records.size(), sum);
    }

    // Read-write mapping
    {
        MappedFile file("/data/output.bin", MapMode::ReadWrite);
        auto region = file.region(0, 1024);
        for (auto& byte : region) {
            byte = std::byte{0xFF};
        }
        file.flush();
    }
    // Automatically unmapped here
}
```
